topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of old paper and faint lavender, usually comforting in our shared room, suddenly felt suffocating. I was halfway through sketching in my journal, a private landscape of anxieties and dreams, when I glanced up. Liam was sitting on his bed, a book open on his lap, his gaze fixed intently on the pages I’d just turned. My breath hitched. I slowly lowered the book, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to appear nonchalant. "Hey, what are you reading?" I managed, my voice coming out a little too high. Liam looked up, a slow, unreadable smile spreading across his face. "Just catching up on some philosophy," he replied smoothly, but his eyes lingered for a fraction of a second too long on my open notebook. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I quickly slammed my journal shut, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet room. I needed to leave. Now.

[story 2]

My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo against the silence of the dorm room. I crept over to my desk, peering around Liam's shoulder. The page I’d been writing on—a raw outpouring of feelings about my impending presentation—was visible, illuminated by the weak desk lamp. His finger, long and slender, was tracing a line of text. Every muscle in my body tensed; I felt a desperate urge to bolt for the door. When I finally forced myself to speak, my voice sounded thin and reedy. "Liam, please stop," I whispered, the plea laced with a tremor I couldn't control. He looked up, startled, and immediately snapped the book shut, his expression shifting into something guarded. The air between us thickened, heavy and charged. I backed away slowly, my eyes darting towards the door, waiting for an escape route that seemed miles away.

[story 3]

I froze mid-sentence, my pen hovering over the page. It was a horrifying realization: Liam was reading my thoughts. My skin prickled with an intense, prickly awareness. I could feel the scrutiny, the silent invasion of my most private space. I slowly turned my head, trying to look casual, but my eyes betrayed me—wide, fixed on him. He hadn't moved, just held the book steady, a quiet intensity radiating from him that made the room feel suddenly too small. A wave of sheer alarm washed over me, making my hands shake uncontrollably. "Are you… are you looking at that?" I managed, the words barely escaping my throat. The silence stretched taut, filled only by the frantic thumping inside my own chest.
